sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, showing inconsistency but a decent defensive record with only 2 goals conceded in league play. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing only 1 win in their last 5 matches, with 2 losses and 1 draw in league games, indicating a dip in form with just 2 goals scored.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, matches have been closely contested, with one draw (0-0) and one narrow win for Atlético Madrid (2-1). These games suggest tight battles with low-scoring outcomes and a high likelihood of cards, as seen in past statistics.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés have shown defensive solidity (4th in least goals conceded) and decent possession stats (56.5%), but struggle to convert chances (40% conversion rate). Atlético Madrid, despite their league position (15th), have similar attacking output but lack consistency in creating big chances, which could impact their performance away from home.

sections.subtitle: Match Context
sections.text: Given Alavés’ home advantage and Atlético’s recent struggles on the road, a low-scoring draw seems plausible. Both teams’ tendency for under 2.5 goals in recent games and head-to-heads supports a cautious approach in prediction.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Deportivo Alavés and Atlético Madrid at Estadio de Mendizorroza ended with a 1-1 scoreline. Both teams showed intent early on, with Atlético Madrid striking first through Giuliano Simeone in the 7th minute, only for Alavés to respond swiftly with a penalty converted by Carlos Vicente in the 14th minute. The game has been evenly contested, with Alavés holding a slight edge in possession and creating a big chance, while Atlético Madrid have been more active in the penalty area with more entries. Both sides have shown moments of promise but also areas of vulnerability that could be exploited in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés have displayed a balanced approach, maintaining slightly more possession and recording a higher expected goals figure compared to their opponents. Their entries into the final third have been frequent, though they’ve struggled to win back possession in dangerous areas. Defensively, they’ve been relatively solid, with several clearances from their backline. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, have been more direct, entering the penalty area more often and managing a couple of shots on target. Their aerial dominance has been notable, though they’ve picked up more disciplinary cautions, which could affect their aggression in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum and rhythm
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Alavés started with some early pressure but were quickly overshadowed by a sustained spell of dominance from Atlético Madrid, especially around the time of their opening goal. After conceding, Alavés regained control with a significant push leading to their equalizer, showcasing a strong attacking phase in the middle of the half. As the half progressed, Atlético Madrid wrestled back momentum with consistent attacking spells, particularly towards the latter stages. Both teams have had their moments, but neither has maintained a consistent threat for prolonged periods.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Alavés, Carlos Vicente has been the standout performer, confidently slotting home the penalty to level the score and showing composure in attack. His contribution has been vital to their first-half efforts. On the Atlético Madrid side, Giuliano Simeone made an immediate impact with his early goal, proving to be a constant threat upfront. Players like Julián Álvarez and Thiago Almada have also shown glimpses of creativity for Atlético, while Alavés’ defensive unit, including Nahuel Tenaglia, has worked hard to contain the opposition’s advances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see both teams push for a winner, given the tight scoreline and competitive nature of the first 45 minutes. Alavés will aim to capitalize on their possession advantage and turn their final third entries into clearer scoring opportunities. Atlético Madrid, with their direct approach and penalty area presence, might look to exploit any defensive lapses from the home side. Discipline could play a role, especially for Atlético, who will need to avoid further bookings. Tactical adjustments at the break could shift the balance, but expect an open game as both sides chase the lead.
